I spent an hour customizing my character only to fail a dice roll and accidentally push my companion into a chasm.
Working a minimum-wage job in space where my coworker got eaten by a giant spider while I was honking a clown horn.
Just one more turn, I told myself at 10 PM. It is now 6 AM and my peaceful neighbor just threatened me with nuclear weapons.
I built a theme park where the bathrooms are insanely expensive and the main roller coaster flies directly into a lake.
A plumber uses a sentient hat to possess a T-Rex and ride a moped through a realistic human city.
I spent five hours building a ridiculously complex wooden robot just to cross a tiny river.
A guy dressed as a flying mammal who beats up 50 goons without ever actually killing them, supposedly.
I spent more time eating fast food until my character got huge and riding a bicycle off a mountain than following the train.
Clicking on monsters repeatedly to get shiny loot so I can click on bigger monsters slightly faster.
I am supposed to be a stealthy assassin, but I prefer singing sea shanties and blowing up British galleons.
I am just walking in circles while hundreds of weapons automatically fire out of my body at endless swarms of bats.
I tried to build a nice little base, but got absolutely swarmed by a terrifying alien rush in the first three minutes.
A critically acclaimed MMORPG where I spend 90% of my time trying on different outfits in the main city.
I spent my childhood chopping down digital oak trees and getting scammed by someone promising to trim my armor.
After a long day of driving to work, I come home to relax by driving a virtual semi-truck across Germany.
